title: Penury. A Quatrain.
content:
Above his misered embers, gnarled and gray,
 With toil-twitched limbs he bends; around his hut,
 Want, like a hobbling hag, goes night and day,
 Scolding at windows and at doors tight-shut.
meaning:
"Penury. A Quatrain." is a quintessential example of Madison Julius Cawein's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
